title: Armenia's Corruption Perception Index decreased by one point: Transparency International
content: Armenia's Corruption Perception Index (CPI) score for 2025 decreased by one point compared to the previous year, dropping to 46 on a scale of 0 (highly corrupt) to 100 (very clean). Transparency International published the 2025 CPI results showing corruption is deepening worldwide. Armenia's score is the average of 6 sources including the Bertelsmann Foundation's 2026 Transformation Index (51 points) and the Freedom House Nations in Transit 2024 report's Corruption sub-index (44 points). In the CPI ranking of 182 countries, Armenia shares 65th-69th places with Kuwait, Montenegro, Namibia, and Senegal (in 2024, Armenia shared 63rd-64th places among 180 countries). Among Council of Europe member states, Armenia shares 30th-31st places with Montenegro, and only three EU member countries (Romania, Bulgaria, and Hungary) have CPI scores lower than Armenia's. Armenia's CPI score (46) is higher than the global average which fell from 43 to 42 — the lowest in the last decade. Among neighbors: Turkey scored 31 (124th-129th places), Iran 23 (153rd-156th places), Azerbaijan 30 (130th-134th places). Among EAEU countries: Kazakhstan 38, Belarus 31, Kyrgyzstan 26, Russia 22.

title: Suspect Donations and Unidentified Sources of the Ruling Civil Contract's Election Campaign Finances
content: An investigation found that transactions associated with the election campaign of the ruling Civil Contract party are suspicious, with some finances having undisclosed origins. A woman named Karine unwillingly became one of the donors of the Civil Contract's election campaign on the eve of the 2023 Yerevan Council of Elders elections. Her ID card and signature were exploited to channel funds of unverified origin into the Civil Contract party's account. The legal maximum donation allowed per person is 2.5 million AMD (approximately 6,200 USD). A significant 87% of contributions to the Civil Contract party's election campaign were amassed through transfers exceeding 1 million AMD. Within this category, around 70% of donations were made by 140 individuals, each contributing the maximum allowable amount of 2.5 million AMD. Three suspicious patterns were identified: individuals contributing donations ranging from 1 to 2.5 million AMD reside in addresses very close to each other; individuals affiliated with large business companies made substantial donations; and prominent contributors include officials, municipal council candidates and current members, and people affiliated with them. Syuzanna Sayadyan, a member of the Council of Elders of the Artashat community, contributed 2.5 million AMD, despite her declared income for 2022 amounting to only 702,975 AMD — received from the Civil Contract party as a salary. One resident of Nor Nork who served as a proxy for the Civil Contract party stated he had transferred 1 million AMD, but the list indicated 2.5 million AMD under his name. The findings conclude that an organized mechanism was employed to channel funds into financing the election campaign of the Civil Contract party, involving associating sums with names of individuals who were often unaware of the process.

title: Four Years Since the Second Nagorno-Karabakh War: A Data-Driven Reflection on Escalations Between Armenia and Azerbaijan
content: It has been four years since the Second Nagorno-Karabakh War erupted, yet the region continues to witness significant escalations along the Armenia-Azerbaijan border. As with any modern armed conflict, the confrontation unfolds not only on the ground but also in the information space. At times, information warfare becomes even more critical and can significantly impact the capacities of both sides. The data-driven analysis reveals that Azerbaijan consistently dominated the information space, issuing more detailed reports on escalations and military actions fine-tuned for external audiences, compared to Armenia. Most peaks in news coverage related to escalations, such as the 2020 war and the 2023 military operation in Nagorno Karabakh, are dominated by Azerbaijan in the number of reports. Armenia only surpassed Azerbaijan in a few notable instances: during border escalations in May 2021, clashes in September 2022, and near Sotk in May 2023. Azerbaijan's major escalations are often preceded by smaller-scale military actions or information campaigns. These may aim to shape public opinion, justify military actions, mask troops' movements, or divert attention from the planned direction of attack. Every major military action by Azerbaijan was preceded by joint Azerbaijan-Turkey exercises on Azerbaijani territory, evidenced by the presence of Turkish military units, particularly F-16 fighter jets and Bayraktar drones, in Azerbaijan during the Second Nagorno-Karabakh War. Authored by Iurii Agafonov, Research Fellow at the Yerevan Center for International Education (YCIE), Scrapalyze Lab.

title: Foreign Minister of Armenia Ararat Mirzoyan received the Secretary General of BSEC Lazar Comanescu
content: Foreign Minister of Armenia Ararat Mirzoyan received the Secretary General of the Organization of the Black Sea Economic Cooperation (BSEC), Lazar Comanescu, as reported by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Armenia. The interlocutors noted that this year marks the 30th anniversary of the establishment of BSEC and emphasized the further development of the organization. Foreign Minister Mirzoyan stressed the importance of expanding sectoral cooperation with BSEC, emphasizing that it will contribute to the implementation of the Government's programs aimed at ensuring sustainable economic growth through regional economic inclusive cooperation. Secretary-General Comanescu commended Armenia's active work and contribution to the development of BSEC. Minister Mirzoyan expressed conviction that the effective implementation of BSEC's main objectives, based on depoliticized, inclusive regional economic cooperation and trade facilitation, the inadmissibility of the policy of blockade and closed borders, are important for regional stability and peace.

title: Stefano Tomat presented to Armen Grigoryan the works to be carried out by the EU mission in Armenia
content: On February 21, Security Council chief of staff Armen Grigoryan received the delegation headed by Stefano Tomat, commander of civil operations of the EU External Action Service. Welcoming the arrival and official launch of the new EU civilian monitoring mission in Armenia, the Security Council Secretary expressed confidence that it will continue to contribute to the establishment of stability and peace in the region. Tomat thanked the Armenian side for their support of the EU civil mission and presented the works to be carried out in Armenia. On January 23, the EU Council reached an agreement on creating a European Union civilian mission in Armenia. The mission started its work on February 20. EEAS Managing Director Stefano Tomat will serve as the Civilian Operation Commander, while Markus Ritter will serve as the Head of Mission. Through its deployment on the Armenian side of the Armenia-Azerbaijan border, the objectives are to contribute to stability in the border areas of Armenia, build confidence and human security in conflict-affected areas, and ensure an environment conducive to the normalization efforts between Armenia and Azerbaijan supported by the EU. The total exclusively civilian staff of the EUMA will be approximately 100, including around 50 unarmed observers. The Operational Headquarters of the mission will be in Yeghegnadzor, in Armenia's Vayots Dzor province.

title: The delegation of the EU technical assessment mission was in the border settlements of Vardenis
content: On October 18, the delegation of the EU technical assessment mission, along with the commander of the second army corps of the RA Armed Forces, colonel Vahram Grigoryan, and the head of the Vardenis community, Aharon Khachatryan, was accompanied to the border zones of the Tretuk and Kutakan settlements of the Vardenis community, which were affected by the Azerbaijani aggression of 2022.

title: Armenia proposes Iran-Black Sea corridor for Indian traders
content: Armenia has proposed a Persian Gulf-Black Sea corridor to connect Indian traders with Russia and Europe, as reported by The Economic Times. Armenia plans to start negotiations with India to discuss the involvement of Indian funding and Indian companies in the project.

title: The Economist published the rating of Russia's allies: Armenia is in the second place
content: The Economist published an article entitled 'Russia's friends are a motley and shrinking crew', presenting the rating of Moscow's strategic allies called 'Putin's Pal's Index'. The list includes 12 countries: Belarus is first, Armenia is second. Kyrgyzstan, Iran, Tajikistan, Kazakhstan, Syria, China, Venezuela, Nicaragua, India, and Uzbekistan are also included. Russia has five official allies in the Collective Security Treaty Organization (CSTO) — Armenia, Belarus, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, and Tajikistan — all obliged to help each other if attacked. However, none supported Russia with troops in the war against Ukraine. Belarus provides only its territory as a military base. Armenia was noted as being upset that the 3,500 Russian soldiers on its territory did not help during the war with Azerbaijan in 2020. Armenia refused that year's CSTO exercises and appeared to be seeking alternative political patrons by inviting the EU mission to monitor the border with Azerbaijan. The Economist concluded that diplomatically, Russia seems almost isolated.

title: Armen Grigoryan and Alexander Vinnikov discussed the current programs of the RA-NATO partnership
content: On October 12, Secretary of the Security Council Armen Grigoryan met with Alexander Vinnikov, head of the NATO Liaison Office in Georgia. The interlocutors discussed current RA-NATO partnership programs and possibilities of deepening cooperation in democratic reforms of the armed forces.

title: On one hand, Aliyev keeps stating that they're ready to talk with NK Armenians, on the other hand, they attack - Edmon Marukyan
content: RA Ambassador-at-Large Edmon Marukyan referred to the Armenian-Azerbaijani conflict, noting that Azerbaijan violated the ceasefire in Nagorno Karabakh. He stated that on one hand Aliyev keeps stating readiness to talk with NK Armenians while on the other hand they attack.

title: There will be some actions, there will be answers - Zakharova announced that Russian peacekeepers will respond to the appearance of representatives of the EU mission
content: Maria Zakharova, the representative of the Russian Foreign Ministry, announced that Russian peacekeepers will respond to the appearance of representatives of the EU mission in the border regions of Armenia.

title: Azerbaijan intends to close the Lachin Corridor - Alen Simonyan
content: Simonyan stated that Azerbaijan intended to close the Lachin Corridor, which he described as the only full-fledged road of humanitarian significance for the livelihood of the Armenians of Nagorno-Karabakh. He emphasized that since the last session, the military-political situation in the South Caucasus had worsened, detailing military operations by Azerbaijan's armed forces that invaded Armenia's territory in September. He emphasized that the belligerent rhetoric of official Baku testifies to Azerbaijan's future expansionist ambitions. The speech was given on December 5 at the joint session of the Council of the Parliamentary Assembly of the CSTO and at the 15th plenary session. Since December 12, 2022, Azerbaijan had closed the Lachin Corridor, resulting in 120,000 residents of Artsakh, including 30,000 children, being under siege, with 400 tons of daily food and medicine unable to be delivered from Armenia. On February 28, 2023, Simonyan stated that the closure of the Lachin corridor inflicted greater harm on Azerbaijan than on Armenia and Artsakh politically.

title: Details regarding the request of the European Court to apply interim measures against Azerbaijan submitted by the RA government
content: The European Court decided to oblige Azerbaijan to take measures to provide safe movement through the Lachin Corridor for seriously ill persons needing medical care in Armenia. Armenia was working to raise Azerbaijan's non-compliance with the ICJ ruling at the UN Security Council, after the International Court of Justice ordered Azerbaijan on February 22 to take all steps at its disposal to ensure unimpeded movement along the Lachin Corridor, which had been blocked since December 12, 2022.

title: Ararat Mirzoyan and the Egyptian Foreign Minister emphasized the importance of maintaining the attention of the international community to the region
content: Armenian Foreign Minister Ararat Mirzoyan, invited to the ministerial session of the Council of the League of Arab States, met with the Foreign Minister of the Arab Republic of Egypt, Sameh Shukri, in Cairo. They expressed satisfaction with the high-level political dialogue between Armenia and Egypt based on centuries-old friendship. Both sides believe the historic visit of the Egyptian president to Armenia in January will be a new impetus for bilateral cooperation. They discussed prospects in trade and economy, information technologies, tourism, and other fields, highlighting the 6th session of the Armenian-Egyptian intergovernmental commission. Mirzoyan presented the latest developments in Armenia-Azerbaijan relations, efforts to establish peace and stability, and the escalation of tension by Azerbaijan in Nagorno-Karabakh.

title: Ararat Mirzoyan met with Jordanian Foreign Minister Ayman al-Safadi
content: Within the framework of the ministerial session of the OECD Council, RA Foreign Minister Ararat Mirzoyan met with Ayman al-Safadi, Deputy Prime Minister of the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan, Minister of Foreign Affairs and Diaspora Affairs, in Cairo on March 8. They discussed issues related to the Armenian-Jordanian bilateral agenda and prospects for developing mutually beneficial cooperation. They emphasized high-level mutual visits and active interaction on international platforms. RA Foreign Minister presented efforts in normalizing Armenia-Azerbaijan relations, establishing peace and stability in the region. Referring to Azerbaijan's blocking of the Lachin Corridor, Minister Mirzoyan emphasized the resolution of the humanitarian situation in Nagorno-Karabakh.

title: The EU monitoring mission had a document that has limited access - Mirzoyan
content: Armenian Foreign Minister Ararat Mirzoyan discussed the EU monitoring mission and noted that the mission had a document that has limited access.

title: Turkish myths about the Armenian nuclear power plant
content: An article by Anna Sahakyan about Turkish narratives and misconceptions regarding the Armenian nuclear power plant (Metsamor). The power unit 2 of the Armenian Nuclear Power Plant operates in Armenia, put into operation in 1980. The design operation lifetime was until September 2016, and before then it was planned to build a new nuclear power unit, which was postponed because of the Fukushima disaster and the global financial crisis.

title: Reshuffle in General Staff won't cause any problems or threats, says Minister of Defense
content: The Armed Forces of Armenia have entered an era of serious reforms. Minister of Defense Suren Papikyan said in parliament it is high time to entrust numerous battle-hardened, capable and young officials with high positions. He said that taking into consideration the developments in the region and around the world, the reforms in the military are a necessity and delaying is inadmissible. The defense budget of 2022 was 11% more than the 2021 plan, amounting to more than 345 billion drams. Prime Minister Pashinyan stated: We will consistently move along the path of creating a professional army in Armenia.

title: Peace agenda has broad public consensus, says MP Lena Nazaryan
content: Member of Parliament from the ruling Civil Contract bloc Lena Nazaryan says the peace agenda has a very broad public consensus. Speaking in parliament, Nazaryan said she sees the consensus while meeting with citizens and families of fallen troops. She stated that the Pashinyan Administration, which is advancing the peace agenda, is the most progressive. She said the parliamentary opposition is continuously rejecting the government's offers to meet and receive answers to all questions, accusing the opposition of being a pseudo-opposition.

title: The Fair Armenia party issues an official statement on parliamentary elections
content: The Fair Armenia party reaffirmed that the judicial system is the Achilles' heel of the state and society, stating they are working on a complete transformation of the judiciary with an irreversible determination to build an impartial, honest, and truly uncorrupt judiciary. They emphasized that the creation of an environment of justice in all state institutions is imperative and a requirement of the time.

title: Professor Armen Charchyan detained by police
content: The representative of the Armenia alliance reported that Professor Armen Charchyan, Director of Izmirlyan Medical Centre, was detained by Police. The alliance called it explicit political persecution reaching the ultimate stage of absurdity for which they will be held accountable.

title: Daily News Summary [12.03.2022]
content: Daily news digest covering key events and developments in Armenia as of March 12, 2022.

title: Meeting between President Vahagn Khachaturyan and President of Georgia Salome Zourabichvili took place
content: President Vahagn Khachaturyan of Armenia and President Salome Zourabichvili of Georgia held a meeting to discuss bilateral relations and regional cooperation.

title: There is no discussion or agreement on the redemarcation of the Armenian-Turkish border - RA MFA spox
content: The spokesperson of the Republic of Armenia Ministry of Foreign Affairs stated that there is no discussion or agreement on the redemarcation of the Armenian-Turkish border.

title: Armenian cemeteries in all settlements of Hadrut are being demolished and destroyed
content: The Human Rights Defender of Artsakh reported that Armenian cemeteries in all settlements of Hadrut are being demolished and destroyed and the gravestones are used as building material for roads.

title: It's unacceptable for us the more and more visible practice of the Russian peacekeeping troops to become a silent witness to the depopulation of Nagorno Karabakh - Pashinyan
content: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan stated that it is unacceptable for Armenia the more and more visible practice of the Russian peacekeeping troops becoming a silent witness to the depopulation of Nagorno-Karabakh. This highlights growing tensions between Armenia and Russia over Russia's failure to fulfill its peacekeeping mandate in the region.

title: Nikol Pashinyan spoke at the UN about the return of Armenian POWs, unblocking roads and establishing peace in the region
content: In a speech at the United Nations, Prime Minister Pashinyan referred to the situation in the South Caucasus, noting that in the fall of 2020 Nagorno-Karabakh was subjected to aggression and a forty-four day war took several thousands of lives. He said: These and other steps are taken to demonstrate the impossibility of peace in our region, but we will consistently advance that agenda by using every opportunity and by creating new opportunities to open an era of peace for our region.

title: Nikol Pashinyan meets with Maia Sandu – the sides highlight development of economic relations between Armenia and Moldova
content: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan met with Moldovan President Maia Sandu, with both sides highlighting the development of economic relations between Armenia and Moldova.

title: Pashinyan sent a congratulatory message to Zelensky on the New Year and Christmas holidays
content: Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan sent a congratulatory message to Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on the New Year and Christmas holidays.

title: Ararat Mirzoyan participated in the reception given by Joe Biden
content: Armenian Foreign Minister Ararat Mirzoyan participated in the reception given by US President Joe Biden.

title: HRD Kristinne Grigoryan submitted a resignation letter to the National Assembly
content: Human Rights Defender Kristinne Grigoryan submitted a resignation letter to the National Assembly of Armenia.

title: This blockage, which is turning into a humanitarian crisis, must be ended as soon as possible - Garo Palyan
content: Garo Palyan emphasized that the blockage of the Lachin Corridor is turning into a humanitarian crisis and must be ended as soon as possible. Since December 12, 2022, 120,000 residents of Artsakh including 30,000 children have been under siege with 400 tons of daily food and medicine unable to be delivered from Armenia.

title: The territorial integrity of Azerbaijan has nothing to do with NKR and its status - Artsakh MFA statement
content: The Artsakh Ministry of Foreign Affairs issued a statement saying that the territorial integrity of Azerbaijan has nothing to do with the Nagorno-Karabakh Republic and its status. The statement was in response to claims regarding the Almaty declaration and the status of Artsakh.

title: Referring to the Almaty declaration does not mean that there will be no opportunity to refer to the status of Artsakh - Aghajanyan
content: Aghajanyan stated that referring to the Almaty declaration does not mean that there will be no opportunity to refer to the status of Artsakh, discussing the legal and diplomatic implications of the Almaty declaration for the Nagorno-Karabakh issue.

title: Opposition has no final decision yet over nominating candidate for President of Armenia
content: The opposition Armenia and I Have the Honor factions discussed nominating a candidate for President of the Republic. MP Gegham Manukyan stated that discussions continue on the matter.

title: Our goal is to prevent any new concessions at any cost - Ishkhan Saghatelyan
content: Ishkhan Saghatelyan discussed opposition plans including a rally in the first ten days of November, stating the goal is to prevent any new concessions at any cost. The rally plans became a subject of heated discussions on social media.

title: The talks about the Union state of Armenia with Russia and Belarus are premature - Narek Sukiasyan
content: Narek Sukiasyan stated that the talks about the Union state of Armenia with Russia and Belarus are premature, discussing the prospects and challenges of deeper integration within the EAEU framework.

title: Lavrov and Bayramov discussed the current situation in the region, including the implementation of tripartite statements
content: Russian Foreign Minister Lavrov and Azerbaijani Foreign Minister Bayramov discussed the current situation in the region, including the implementation of tripartite statements regarding the Nagorno-Karabakh conflict settlement.

title: Armenian, Russian FMs discuss regional developments
content: Armenian and Russian Foreign Ministers discussed regional developments, covering bilateral relations and the situation in the South Caucasus.

title: PM Pashinyan holds separate meetings with the heads of Sada and Service Titan companies operating in the field of high technologies
content: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan held separate meetings with the heads of Sada and Service Titan companies operating in the field of high technologies, discussing investment and technology development in Armenia.

title: Daily News Digest [29.10.2021]
content: Daily news digest covering key events and developments in Armenia as of October 29, 2021.

title: #armvote2021 Daily News Digest [05.06.2021]
content: Election-focused daily news digest during the 2021 Armenian parliamentary election campaign, covering events as of June 5, 2021.

title: Financial means of MP candidates: Part Three [I Have Honor alliance]
content: An investigation into the financial declarations of MP candidates from the I Have Honor alliance during the 2021 Armenian parliamentary elections, examining their declared assets and income sources.

title: The construction of 9 houses almost completed in Shurnukh
content: The construction of 9 houses in the border village of Shurnukh is nearly complete, addressing housing needs in the settlement affected by border changes following the 2020 war.

title: The Azerbaijanis closed the Lachin corridor again
content: Azerbaijan closed the Lachin Corridor again, cutting off the only lifeline connecting Nagorno-Karabakh to Armenia, affecting 120,000 residents including 30,000 children.

title: Fact-Finding Commission on Non-Combat Deaths and Post-Election Persecutions
content: The topic of non-combat deaths is one of the issues that the state decided to investigate within the framework of the Fact-Finding Commission. Among these issues are also national and local self-government elections and referendums, post-election political persecutions, violations of the right to freedom of assembly, expropriations of property under the guise of state needs and public interest, and other manifestations of deprivation of the right to property. The Fact-Finding Commission was set to be formed and begin its activities in the first quarter of 2025. The article references the case of Nana Muradyan, whose son's right to life and effective investigation violation was recorded by the European Court of Human Rights. She is skeptical about the upcoming investigation. Her son Valerik Muradyan's body was found hanging in a military unit on March 15, 2010, with the official version being suicide.

title: Electricity planned outages in Yerevan and regions
content: Planned electricity outages were announced for a number of addresses in Yerevan and the regions of Armenia.
